role_title: Associate, New Verticals - Alcohol Strategy & Operations

level_signals: 1-3+ years' experience in consulting, strategy, tech or related experience
Base range $74,800–$110,000 consistent with early-career associate level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 1-3+ years' experience in consulting, strategy, tech or related experience
Build models using tools like Excel and SQL to evaluate the economic and strategic costs and benefits of various programs and experiments
Own tracking dashboards and manage week over week metrics to drive safe, compliant delivery operations.
Define market expansion strategy: which markets to enter and when
Partner with many teams like: Operations, Product, Engineering, Legal, Policy, Comms, etc. to create strategic plans and harness opportunities while gaining buy-in from stakeholders and leadership
You have a bias for action and are able to thrive in a fast-paced, constantly changing environment
You have a talent for synthesizing complex problems, can translate high-level goals into actionable plans, and enjoy implementing a process

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: high-growth or early-stage start-up experience or compliance experience is a bonus

scope.surface: Alcohol & Regulated Goods team overseeing a $2B annual revenue business spanning alcohol, hemp, and THC; focus on complex/regulated markets for same-day delivery
scope.business_model: B2B2C marketplace — three-sided marketplace (Merchants, Dashers, Consumers)
primary_metrics_inferred: Week-over-week operational metrics on compliance and fulfillment
Customer experience quality (order tracking, fulfillment expectations)
Marketplace dynamics (minimum order values, selection quality)
core_responsibilities: Lead expansion and operations of the alcohol business in markets with unique regulatory or operational complexities
Define market expansion strategy: which markets to enter and when
Develop tailored commercial terms and partnership packages
Identify and prioritize key retailers to onboard
Build and scale operational processes to support growth in complex markets
Improve customer experience (e.g., enhanced order tracking, clearer fulfillment expectations)
Optimize marketplace dynamics (e.g., minimum order values, selection quality)
Own tracking dashboards and manage week over week metrics
Build models using Excel and SQL to evaluate economic and strategic costs and benefits

green_flags: Clear business scope: $2B annual revenue alcohol and regulated goods business
Well-defined pre-sales and post-sales responsibility split
Strong cross-functional exposure (Operations, Product, Engineering, Legal, Policy, Comms)
Comprehensive benefits package described including 16 weeks parental leave, 401(k) matching
red_flags: Location type is unclear — JD body does not specify city, remote, or hybrid arrangement; NYC AEDT notice suggests some NYC relevance but role location not explicitly defined
Equity not mentioned in compensation section — unusual for a tech company at this level
1-3+ years range is somewhat ambiguous — the '+' after '3' could imply more senior candidates are also acceptable
